howItWorks.intro: This slope-intercept form calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is slope-intercept form?
faq.answer: Slope-intercept form is y = mx + b, where m is the slope of the line and b is the y-intercept (where the line crosses the y-axis). It is the most common way to write a linear equation.

faq.question: How do I find b (the y-intercept)?
faq.answer: Once you know the slope m, plug one of your points into y = mx + b and solve for b. So b = y₁ - m·x₁.

faq.question: Can a vertical line be written in slope-intercept form?
faq.answer: No. A vertical line has an undefined slope, so it cannot be written as y = mx + b. Instead, it is written as x = a constant.

faq.question: What does the y-intercept tell you?
faq.answer: The y-intercept b is the value of y when x = 0. Graphically, it is where the line crosses the y-axis.
